title: Scalable and Efficient Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Model Checker for Computation Tree Logic
authors: Ghalya Alwhishi, Jamal Bentahar, Amine Andam, Ahmed Elwhishi, Mustapha Hedabou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Formal verification using temporal logics such as computation tree logic (CTL) is essential for validating safety and correctness in complex systems. However, traditional model-checking techniques face severe scalability limitations due to the state explosion problem and their reliance on exhaustive symbolic traversal. Moreover, existing learning-based verification methods often lack formal guarantees and interpretability. These challenges create a pressing need for scalable, learning-based verification methods that preserve verification reliability while improving computational efficiency. This article introduces a novel deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based model checking framework that learns to verify CTL formulas directly through interaction with system models. Unlike traditional symbolic model checkers such as NuSMV, the proposed DRL-CTL checker trained using proximal policy optimization (PPO) interprets CTL semantics over system models represented as Kripke structures without performing symbolic state-space traversal at inference time. Reward functions are designed for individual CTL operators, and fixed-point reasoning is incorporated to handle global temporal properties such as <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$AG(\phi)$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$EG(\phi)$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves near-constant inference time of approximately 2 ms per formula on an Intel Core i9-13900K CPU (24 cores, 3.0 GHz), 64 GB RAM, NVIDIA RTX 4090 GPU (24 GB VRAM), reduces verification time by up to 90% compared with traditional model checkers, and scales to models with more than <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{1192}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> reachable states. The framework also produces witnesses and counterexamples and yields verification outcomes identical to those of symbolic checkers in our experiments. These results highlight the potential of DRL to serve as a scalable, efficient, and explainable alternative to classical CTL model checking.
title_zh: 基于可扩展且高效深度强化学习的计算树逻辑模型检测器
abstract_zh:
使用计算树逻辑（CTL）等时序逻辑进行形式化验证，对于确保复杂系统的安全性与正确性至关重要。然而，传统的模型检测技术由于状态爆炸问题以及对符号化遍历的依赖，面临着严重的可扩展性限制。此外，现有的基于学习的验证方法通常缺乏形式化保证和可解释性。这些挑战迫切需要一种在保持验证可靠性的同时提升计算效率的、可扩展的基于学习的验证方法。

本文提出了一种新颖的基于深度强化学习（DRL）的模型检测框架，该框架通过与系统模型的交互，直接学习如何验证CTL公式。与NuSMV等传统符号模型检测器不同，所提出的DRL-CTL检测器采用近端策略优化（PPO）进行训练，在推理阶段无需对状态空间进行符号化遍历，而是直接在以Kripke结构表示的系统模型上解释CTL语义。我们为各个CTL操作符设计了相应的奖励函数，并引入不动点推理机制以处理诸如 $AG(\phi)$ 和 $EG(\phi)$ 等全局时序性质。

实验结果表明，该方法在配备Intel Core i9-13900K CPU（24核，3.0 GHz）、64 GB内存和NVIDIA RTX 4090 GPU（24 GB显存）的平台上，每条公式的推理时间稳定在约2毫秒，相比传统模型检测器最多可减少90%的验证时间，并能够扩展至具有超过 $10^{1192}$ 个可达状态的系统模型。该框架还能生成验证路径（witnesses）和反例（counterexamples），且在实验中产生的验证结果与符号检测器完全一致。这些结果表明，DRL有望成为经典CTL模型检测的一种可扩展、高效且具备可解释性的替代方案。

title: FocusFuzz: Towards Efficient and Directed Fuzzing for RTL Processor Designs
authors: He Wang, Ruomin Fang, Jingqian Zhang, Xu Chen, Qizhen Xu, Ruiping Yin, Liwei Chen, Gang Shi
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: As processor architectures evolve with the integration of new functional units, verifying the entire hardware design after each modification becomes increasingly inefficient. Although a significant portion of the modules remains unmodified between iterations, traditional verification methodologies still require re-testing of the entire process, failing to leverage this inherent modularity in hardware development.To address this inefficiency, we propose FocusFuzz, a novel module-directed hardware fuzzing framework tailored for processor verification. FocusFuzz integrates two key components: a Static-Dynamic Module Distance Profiler(SDMD) that identifies modules functionally close to the target, and a Module Feature-Guided Mutation Engine (MFGM) enhanced by a lightweight LSTM model to adapt mutation strategies to module-specific functional characteristics. We evaluated FocusFuzz on open-source RISC-V cores, where at the module level it improves time-to-target- coverage (TTTC) by up to 89× and achieves an average 6.94× speedup over DifuzzRTL, the famous currently available fuzzer.
title_zh: FocusFuzz：面向高效且定向的RTL处理器设计模糊测试
abstract_zh: 随着处理器架构不断集成新的功能单元，每次修改后对整个硬件设计进行验证变得越来越低效。尽管在迭代过程中大部分模块保持不变，但传统的验证方法仍需重新测试整个流程，未能充分利用硬件开发中固有的模块化特性。为解决这一效率低下的问题，我们提出了FocusFuzz——一种新颖的、面向模块的硬件模糊测试框架，专用于处理器验证。FocusFuzz包含两个核心组件：静态-动态模块距离分析器（SDMD），用于识别在功能上与目标模块相近的其他模块；以及模块特征引导的变异引擎（MFGM），该引擎结合轻量级LSTM模型，根据各模块特定的功能特征自适应地调整变异策略。我们在开源RISC-V核上对FocusFuzz进行了评估，结果表明，在模块级别上，其达到目标覆盖率的时间（TTTC）最快可提升达89倍，平均比当前著名的模糊测试工具DifuzzRTL快6.94倍。

title: Multi-Classifier and Deep Learning Framework for Robust Credit Card Fraud Detection
authors: Swathi D R, Kalaiarasan T, Sanjai C, Vinotheni M S, Mariammal K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: Credit card fraud remains a major concern in the financial sector, leading to significant financial losses and eroding customer trust. Real-time detection is challenging due to highly imbalanced datasets and constantly changing fraud behaviors. This work presents a unified fraud detection framework that combines supervised learning models such as Random Forest, Stochastic Gradient Descent Classifier, Support Vector Machine and Logistic Regression with unsupervised techniques including Isolation Forest, One-Class Support Vector Machine and Density-Based Spatial Clustering for Applications with Noise to identify both known and previously unseen fraud patterns. Reinforcement learning methods such as Q-Learning and Deep Q-Learning enable adaptive detection strategies that improve over time, while deep learning architectures such as Multilayer Perceptron and Long Short-Term Memory networks capture complex sequential and nonlinear transaction patterns. A key contribution is the extraction and conversion of model parameters into FPGA compatible formats using Vitis High-Level Synthesis, enabling real-time hardware-accelerated inference with significantly reduced latency. Experimental evaluation on benchmark datasets demonstrates superior accuracy, recall, F1-score and ROC-AUC with lower false-positive rates compared to individual models, making the proposed framework scalable, adaptive and effective for real-time credit card fraud detection..
title_zh: 用于鲁棒信用卡欺诈检测的多分类器与深度学习框架
abstract_zh: 信用卡欺诈仍然是金融领域的一大难题，导致重大财务损失并削弱客户信任。由于数据集高度不平衡且欺诈行为不断变化，实时检测面临巨大挑战。本文提出了一种统一的欺诈检测框架，该框架结合了随机森林、随机梯度下降分类器、支持向量机和逻辑回归等监督学习模型，以及孤立森林、一类支持向量机和基于密度的空间聚类算法（DBSCAN）等无监督技术，以识别已知及此前未见过的欺诈模式。通过Q学习和深度Q学习等强化学习方法，实现可随时间优化的自适应检测策略；同时，多层感知机和长短期记忆网络等深度学习架构能够捕捉复杂的序列性和非线性交易模式。本研究的一个关键贡献在于利用Vitis高层次综合（HLS）技术提取模型参数并将其转换为兼容FPGA的格式，从而实现硬件加速的实时推理，显著降低延迟。在基准数据集上的实验评估表明，与单一模型相比，所提出的框架在准确率、召回率、F1分数和ROC-AUC方面表现更优，且误报率更低，具备良好的可扩展性、适应性和高效性，适用于实时信用卡欺诈检测。

title: RISC-V Microarchitecture Information Leakage Attack via Transient Execution
authors: Jiashun Wang, Rundi Zhai, Yishuo Wang, Chaoyue Liang, Baojiang Cui
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: The increasing threat of microarchitectural transient attacks in recent years has presented a significant challenge to processor security. Immediate measures are essential to address the microarchitectural security vulnerabilities in RISC-V processors. This paper systematically evaluates seven commercially available RISC-V processors. The proposed attack framework comprehensively analyzes cache security and timing side-channel attacks across various microarchitectures. Additionally, it introduces three transient execution attack primitives targeting the RISC-V microarchitecture, including a previously undiscovered sub-variant. We implement proof-of-concept attacks on seven distinct microarchitectures and validate their feasibility and effectiveness. Experimental results show that unprivileged attackers can illicitly access sensitive data on real-world RISC-V processors using timing side-channel attacks and transient execution attack primitives. Furthermore, the attack demonstrates a high degree of accuracy, successfully compromising seven microarchitectures with a success rate of approximately 96% and a 30% improvement in efficiency. We reported two CVE vulnerabilities, both of which were confirmed by the developers, highlighting the practicality of the security analysis.
title_zh: 通过瞬态执行的RISC-V微架构信息泄露攻击
abstract_zh: 近年来，微架构瞬态攻击的威胁日益加剧，给处理器安全带来了重大挑战。为应对RISC-V处理器中的微架构安全漏洞，亟需采取紧急措施。本文系统性地评估了七款商用RISC-V处理器，提出了一种攻击框架，能够全面分析不同微架构下的缓存安全性和时序侧信道攻击。此外，本文还提出了三种针对RISC-V微架构的瞬态执行攻击原语，其中包括一种此前未被发现的子变体。我们在七种不同的微架构上实现了概念验证攻击，并验证了其可行性和有效性。实验结果表明，无特权的攻击者可利用时序侧信道攻击和瞬态执行攻击原语，非法获取现实世界中RISC-V处理器上的敏感数据。该攻击具有很高的准确度，在七种微架构上的攻击成功率约为96%，效率提升了30%。我们报告了两个CVE漏洞，均已被开发者确认，凸显了本研究安全分析的实际意义。
